meaning: adj.容易操作的,有机动性的
examples: It is the same speed as the Pitbull, but more maneuverable.
This motorcycle is light, maneuverable, feels great, and is fun to ride.
Smaller cars use less gas, are more maneuverable, cause fewer deaths, use fewer resources.
The roof is designed to be maneuverable and slides to the rear to open.
TerraMax's size and vague resemblance to Optimus Prime don't give it much of an advantage in competition with smaller, more maneuverable cars.
